sentences.english: I wake up when my alarm goes off, which is usually around 6 AM. The first thing I do is check the news on my phone. Then I take a shower to wake up.
sentences.korean: 알람이 울리면 일어나는데 보통 아침 6시쯤이에요. 가장 먼저 폰으로 뉴스를 확인하고, 잠을 깨려고 샤워해요.

sentences.english: After that, I'll make toast with cheese and a cup of tea. I'm not a big breakfast person, but toast and tea gets me going.
sentences.korean: 그 다음 치즈 토스트와 차 한 잔을 만들어요. 아침을 많이 먹는 편은 아니지만, 토스트와 차가 하루를 시작하게 해줘요.

sentences.english: I usually check the news and weather on my computer first thing. Then I take a shower and get dressed. After that, I do a quick workout — maybe 20 minutes of exercise.
sentences.korean: 보통 가장 먼저 컴퓨터로 뉴스와 날씨를 확인해요. 그리고 샤워하고 옷을 입어요. 그 다음 20분 정도 간단한 운동을 해요.

sentences.english: Then I shower again and have some coffee and yogurt. It's a productive morning routine.
sentences.korean: 다시 샤워하고 커피와 요거트를 먹어요. 생산적인 아침 루틴이에요.

sentences.english: I wake up around 7:30 AM. The first thing I do is put the kettle on to make tea. Then I'll have some toast with Vegemite — that's very Australian. I take my time in the morning because I like to start my day slowly and not rush.
sentences.korean: 7시 30분쯤 일어나요. 가장 먼저 차를 끓이려고 주전자를 올려요. 그리고 베지마이트 토스트를 먹어요 — 아주 호주적이죠.

sentences.english: I wake up around 7 AM. The first thing I do is open my window to get fresh air in my room. Then I go to the bathroom and take a shower. The shower really helps me wake up and feel ready for the day ahead.
sentences.korean: 아침 7시쯤 일어나요. 가장 먼저 창문을 열어 방에 신선한 공기를 넣어요. 그리고 화장실에 가서 샤워해요.

sentences.english: My morning routine is simple. I take a shower, get dressed, and have a quick breakfast — maybe cereal or toast. I'm not a morning person, so I try to keep things simple and quick. I don't like to rush, but I also don't have much time.
sentences.korean: 아침 루틴은 간단해요. 샤워하고 옷 입고 간단한 아침 — 시리얼이나 토스트 — 을 먹어요. 아침형 인간은 아니에요.

sentences.english: My mornings are quite hectic. I have a lot of things to do before I leave the house. I wake up, shower, get dressed, and then I'm rushing to get my kids ready for school. It's always chaotic, but that's just how mornings are for me.
sentences.korean: 아침이 꽤 정신없어요. 집을 나서기 전에 할 일이 많거든요.

quiz.question: What does '알람이 울리다' mean in English?
